name: What kind of compensation can I get for falling off a ladder at work?

mainEntity.name: What kind of compensation can I get for falling off a ladder at work?

acceptedAnswer.text: Medical, 2/3 wages, disability from comp; pain/suffering from third-party suits.[2][3]
acceptedAnswer.description:
Workers' comp provides medical bills, lost wages (about 2/3), and disability payments; third-party lawsuits can add pain and suffering damages.[2]

Severe ladder fall injuries like broken bones or herniated discs qualify for ongoing treatment and future lost earnings.[1][3] Values depend on injury severity and income.

- Economic: bills, wages, home mods.
- Non-economic: pain via separate claims.
- Average settlements $50K-$200K+.

Track all expenses; a lawyer evaluates full case worth including GC or owner liability.[2]

description: Medical, 2/3 wages, disability from comp; pain/suffering from third-party suits.[2][3]
